examples.title: Sequential File Read Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates reading records sequentially from a file
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SequentialRead(fileName, targetRecord)
BEGIN
  OPEN fileName FOR READING
  position ← 0
  WHILE NOT EOF(fileName) DO
    READ record FROM fileName
    position ← position + 1
    IF position = targetRecord THEN
      RETURN record
    END IF
  END WHILE
  CLOSE fileName
  RETURN NULL  // Record not found
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm reads records one by one from the beginning until it finds the target record. The file pointer moves sequentially through each record. Time complexity is O(n) where n is the position of the target record.

examples.title: Direct Access Implementation
examples.description: Implements direct access to records using relative record numbers
examples.code:
class DirectAccessFile:
    def __init__(self, filename, record_size):
        self.filename = filename
        self.record_size = record_size
    
    def read_record(self, rrn):
        """Read record at given Relative Record Number"""
        byte_offset = (rrn - 1) * self.record_size
        
        with open(self.filename, 'rb') as f:
            f.seek(byte_offset)
            record = f.read(self.record_size)
        
        return record if record else None
    
    def write_record(self, rrn, data):
        """Write record at given Relative Record Number"""
        byte_offset = (rrn - 1) * self.record_size
        
        with open(self.filename, 'rb+') as f:
            f.seek(byte_offset)
            f.write(data.ljust(self.record_size, b'\x00'))
    
    def delete_record(self, rrn):
        """Mark record as deleted using deletion flag"""
        byte_offset = (rrn - 1) * self.record_size
        
        with open(self.filename, 'rb+') as f:
            f.seek(byte_offset)
            f.write(b'DELETED')
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates direct access using relative record numbers. The byte offset is calculated directly from the RRN, providing O(1) access time. Records can be read, written, or deleted without affecting other records.

examples.title: Indexed Sequential Search
examples.description: Demonstrates indexed access by first searching the index then the data file
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates indexed sequential access. First, a binary search is performed on the index (O(log n)), then direct access is used to read the actual record (O(1)). This provides much faster lookups than pure sequential search for large files.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is index size

examples.title: Buffer Management for File I/O
examples.description: Demonstrates buffering techniques to reduce I/O operations
examples.explanation: This Java code demonstrates buffer management for file I/O. BufferedInputStream reduces physical disk reads by caching data in memory. The BufferManager class shows a simple LRU (Least Recently Used) buffer pool implementation that manages multiple buffers, writes dirty buffers back to disk, and tracks buffer usage for optimal performance.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for buffer reads (when cached), O(n) for disk write
examples.spaceComplexity: O(B × N) where B is buffer size and N is number of buffers
